differential_diagnoses: Adrenal cortisol-secreting tumor (Cushing's syndrome leading to severe immunocompromise)
Paraneoplastic syndrome associated with occult malignancy
Bacterial meningitis (partially treated or atypical pathogen)
next_steps: Immediate initiation of empiric broad-spectrum antifungal therapy (including liposomal amphotericin B and flucytosine)
CSF studies including fungal and bacterial cultures, cryptococcal antigen, and broad-range bacterial/fungal PCR
Serum and 24-hour urine cortisol evaluation to confirm suspected Cushing's syndrome
Assessment of adrenal function and ACTH levels
Serial neurological exams and monitoring of intracranial pressure
Consideration of neurosurgical consultation for ventriculostomy due to elevated opening pressure and hydrocephalus risk

differential_diagnoses: Endogenous Cushing's syndrome (likely ectopic ACTH secretion or adrenal cortical carcinoma)
Disseminated opportunistic fungal infection (e.g., Candida or Histoplasma)
next_steps: Initiate empiric treatment for fungal meningitis (liposomal amphotericin B and flucytosine) and bacterial meningitis (ceftriaxone, vancomycin, and ampicillin)
Perform CSF Cryptococcal antigen testing, India ink preparation, fungal culture, and broad-range PCR
Order serum ACTH, serum DHEAS, and 24-hour urine cortisol (fractionated) to clarify Cushing's etiology
Perform external ventricular drain placement for therapeutic CSF diversion to manage intracranial hypertension
Order contrast-enhanced MRI of the brain with safety precautions for the vagus nerve stimulator to better characterize CNS lesions
Obtain chest and abdominal CT to look for primary occult malignancy
